title: Display a white border around the OWR window for camera tracking
notes:
Going the BepInEx route: write a standalone BepInEx plugin that draws a white frame via Unity IMGUI (OnGUI + DrawTexture). Render the border inside the game itself — no Wayland positioning, no window-decoration tricks. Plugin code in /home/braino/workspace/sinden/SindenBorder/. Compiled .dll lands in OWR BepInEx/plugins/ alongside the DemulShooter plugin.
Verified: 30px white border visible in-game on next OWR launch after dropping SindenBorder.dll into BepInEx/plugins/.

title: Wire Sinden gun event device into OWR plugin via TCP bridge
notes:
Bridge verified end-to-end: reads /dev/input/event24, transforms ABS coords to OWR window coords, sends raw payload to 127.0.0.1:33610. Confirmed crosshair parked at game-window top-left when bridge sent (0,0). Architecture works. Daemon-driven gun events not yet seen because daemon needs a white border on screen to compute valid aim — that's now the blocker (sinden-wq5).
MILESTONE 2026-05-11: User completed the first OWR training level using the Sinden Lightgun on Linux. Full chain works end-to-end: SindenBorder BepInEx plugin draws border → Sinden Mono daemon tracks via camera → gun emits HID events → bridge grabs and forwards via TCP → DemulShooter plugin updates in-game aim. Remaining: jitter (EMA alpha=0.8 not heavy enough), calibration tuning, second-gun player.

title: Map gun front/rear buttons to OWR Heal and Gun-Switch actions
Calibration mode: map gun screen position to OWR window coords
notes:
Bridge supports tunable calibration (--x-min/--x-max/--y-min/--y-max, --y-flip) and EMA smoothing (--smooth) plus outlier rejection (--max-jump, --max-reject-frames). Current good defaults: --x-min 0 --x-max 32767 --y-min 0 --y-max 32767 --smooth 0.5 (no outlier filter needed when actually aiming at screen — outlier filter helped catch false rectangles when gun pointed away from screen). Open follow-ups: tune defaults from gameplay feedback; verify if SindenBorder width=80 (in BepInEx/config/braino.sindenborder.cfg) tracks more reliably than 30.
Calibration verified flawless with: SindenBorder width=150, Sinden daemon MatchOnlyWherePointing=1 + ColourMatchRange=120 (tighter), --x-min 0 --x-max 32767 (direct mapping), --smooth 0.5, --max-jump 0 (outlier filter not needed when gun actually points at screen). Center-of-screen tracking issue fixed by MatchOnlyWherePointing.

title: Pick up SindenBorder config reload — currently requires OWR restart to apply width change
Package as systemd user service that autostarts with OWR
